title: New analytics module — trackEvent.ts not referenced in test-manifest
commit_message: feat: add client-side analytics wrapper with batching

why_dead_zone.explanation: src/analytics/trackEvent.ts does not appear in any test's sources[] entry in test-manifest.json. The test repository was built around reducers, API constants, auth hooks, and utilities — analytics is out of scope. Despite meaningful new behaviour (batching, retry, session correlation), the diff-based selector finds no matching tests.
why_dead_zone.contrast_with_actiotypes: Changing actiotypes.js cascades to many feature tests (see scenario 8 and scenario 24). Adding a brand-new file with no manifest entry produces the opposite: total SKIP.
why_dead_zone.system_output: CRITICAL/HIGH/LOW empty; SKIP = 77; coverage gaps for each new symbol; optional jest command omitted or runs zero tests.

SKIP.reason: src/analytics/trackEvent.ts appears in no sources[] array in test-manifest.json

coverage_gaps.severity: HIGH — new module with no automated safety net
recommended_tests: queues events when batching is enabled
flushAnalyticsQueue sends queued events and clears the queue
does not throw when navigator.sendBeacon is unavailable

coverage_gaps.action_required: Add sources[] entries to test-manifest.json (and tests) before this file can participate in diff-based selection.

jest_run_note: No tests matched — nothing to run. Gap report is the only output.
